name: Ascent to Rocabruna via the Mirador de les Muntanyes de Prades — circular from Aleixar
name_original: Subida a Rocabruna por el Mirador de les Muntanyes de Prades — circular desde Aleixar
region_intro: Hiking around L'Aleixar offers diverse landscapes characterized by extensive fields of hazelnuts, olive trees, and vineyards. The region features rolling hills and elevated viewpoints, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Notable natural features include ancient holm oaks and the presence of dry stone huts, reflecting the area's agricultural heritage. These elements combine to create a rich environment for exploration. Best hiking trails around L'Aleixar The most popular hiking route is Ascent to Rocabruna via the Mirador de les Muntanyes de Prades — circular from Aleixar, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views of the Prades Mountains. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ascent to Rocanys from the Ermita de Sant Blai — circular from Aleixar, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.5 km) path. This trail includes cultural interest points around the Ermita de Sant Blai and leads to elevated viewpoints. Local hikers also love the Puig de la Torre and GR 172 Loop, a 10.9 miles (17.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 46 minutes. This difficult route provides a longer, more challenging experience. Hiking around L'Aleixar is defined by agricultural landscapes, dry stone hut trails, and hills offering panoramic views. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in L'Aleixar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 1,400 hikers have used komoot to explore L'Aleixar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in L'Aleixar?
A: L'Aleixar offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 30 routes available. These trails cater to all skill levels, including 9 easy, 20 moderate, and 3 difficult options.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in L'Aleixar?
A: Yes, L'Aleixar has 9 easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or families. One excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306529193/aleixar-dry-stone-huts-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aleixar Dry Stone Huts Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 6.9 km long and takes about 1 hour 55 minutes to complete. This route allows you to explore the region's agricultural heritage.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in L'Aleixar?
A: The landscapes around L'Aleixar are diverse and picturesque. You'll hike through extensive fields of hazelnuts, olive trees, and vineyards, creating a rich agricultural mosaic. The region also features rolling hills and elevated viewpoints, offering panoramic vistas, especially towards the Prades Mountains.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in L'Aleixar?
A: Yes, many of the trails in L'Aleixar are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306521409/ascent-to-rocabruna-via-the-mirador-de-les-muntanyes-de-prades-circular-from-aleixar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Rocabruna via the Mirador de les Muntanyes de Prades&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.9 km circular route with great views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1309085795/ascent-to-puig-d-en-gullo-circular-from-el-coll-de-la-batalla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Puig d'en Gulló&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.5 km circular path.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: L'Aleixar is home to several interesting natural features. You can explore the dry stone huts that dot the agricultural landscape, reflecting the area's history. For impressive views, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/782537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint over the Cingles de Patxeco&lt;/a&gt; or the summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4744292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rocabruna (539 m)&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts a monumental holm oak, standing 11 meters tall with a diameter of 9.45 meters.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, some trails incorporate cultural interest points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306495410/ascent-to-rocanys-from-the-ermita-de-sant-blai-circular-from-aleixar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Rocanys from the Ermita de Sant Blai&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.5 km route that includes the historic Ermita de Sant Blai, offering both natural beauty and cultural insights.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in L'Aleixar?
A: The hiking trails in L'Aleixar are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 250 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the panoramic views, and the well-maintained paths that showcase the region's unique agricultural and natural beauty.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, L'Aleixar offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1309090555/puig-de-la-torre-and-gr-172-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de la Torre and GR 172 Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 17.6 km trail that typically takes around 5 hours 46 minutes to complete, leading through varied and elevated terrain.

Q: Can I find viewpoints with panoramic scenery in L'Aleixar?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in L'Aleixar offer stunning panoramic views, especially of the Prades Mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306521409/ascent-to-rocabruna-via-the-mirador-de-les-muntanyes-de-prades-circular-from-aleixar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Rocabruna via the Mirador de les Muntanyes de Prades&lt;/a&gt; is specifically known for its breathtaking vistas from the Mirador. You can also find great views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/782537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint over the Cingles de Patxeco&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any caves or unique rock formations to explore?
A: Yes, the region around L'Aleixar features interesting geological formations. You can explore natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/880628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foradada Rock&lt;/a&gt;. There are also caves such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/265083" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Cranes&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/725611" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Avencs de la Febró&lt;/a&gt; for those interested in subterranean exploration.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in L'Aleixar?
A: L'Aleixar's diverse agricultural landscapes, with hazelnuts, olive trees, and vineyards, make it appealing across seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for hiking, with vibrant colors in spring and rich hues in autumn. The region's fertile nature suggests pleasant conditions for outdoor activities for much of the year.

It dates back more than 1000 years, so it could be Arabic. Due to heavy rains in October 1994, she was half-buried.

Rough walls are still preserved; the one to the north is whole enough. It is located to the west of the village, a ten minute walk. It is documented in the minutes book of the Council (1671) when it is agreed to order a bell for the hermitage, or (June 13, 1687) when they agree “that a profaso of prayers be made to Saint Anthony for the plague of the locust and what he preached and that all those who will attend this procession will walk barefoot ”.

Later, and in the book of parish visits, it is ordered that things be put there (1732) and it is stated that they do not allow Mass to be said (1738). But it had to be remedied because on June 13, 1817, "the hermitage of St. Anthony was reconciled, desecrated by French enemy troops and ours in the last war…" (refers to the French war , 1808-1814). However, this normalcy did not last long, because the vicissitudes of the 19th century, the Carlist wars, must have affected it again. In this sense, the Madoz Dictionary, published in 1850, refers to it as "… and a chapel that was also outside the town dedicated to San Antonio, has been destroyed during the calamities of the civil war." A pastoral visit in 1853 only mentions the hermitage of Sant Blai and, in another in 1870, it is said: "the hermitage of San Antonio has not existed for many years in this part".

At the end of the 19th century, a village chaplain, recovering from an illness, proposed to recover and asked the villagers for help. Mr. Lluís Barberà Xatruch, from Mas de Borbó, agreed to collaborate and had wood cut on an old Advent moon, but the initiative was not consolidated and only about twenty years ago the wood was given a new one. purpose. So many years have passed since the hermitage was demolished, which may still be resisted because it has been less than a few years since the trunk kept the possibility of rehabilitation alive.
